messages.content:
Create a retrieval-oriented summary for this parent section of a document.
Use the child summaries below. Mention the topics, entities, dates, tables,
figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, and evidence types covered
when they are visible. Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.

Parent:
{
  "node_id": "0045",
  "title": "2025年游戏获客：真实变革何在",
  "page_index": 30,
  "page_range": "30"
}

Child summaries:
[
  {
    "node_id": "0046",
    "title": "GAMING USER ACQUISITION IN 2025: WHAT ACTUALLY CHANGED 2025年游戏获客：真实变革何在",
    "page_range": "30-31",
    "summary": "**Topics:** 2025 mobile game user acquisition (UA) market evolution, quarterly performance dynamics, genre-specific scale and monetization divergence, cross-platform (Android vs. iOS) marketing strategies, digital ad budget allocation, channel diversification, creative optimization, and advertising measurement maturity/attribution modeling.\n**Entities:** Mobile game advertisers/marketers, top brands/publishers, Singular (data provider/report publisher), Android, iOS, Paid Social platforms, and game genres (casual, hybrid-casual, mid-core, strategy).\n**Dates/Timeframes:** Full year 2025, with granular quarterly breakdowns (Q1–Q4), H2 focus, and the Q4 holiday season. Strategic insights extend into 2026.\n**Definitions/Concepts:** Market transition from an \"emergency state\" to \"self-discipline and standardization\"; \"rational growth path\" prioritizing sustainable value over blind expansion; value-centric optimization methodology (aligning creative, bidding, and channels to early payment propensity signals); channel over-concentration risk; attribution frameworks (Last-Click vs. Multi-Touch Attribution/MTA); Assist Value, Cross-Channel Impact, Modeled Conversions, Hybrid Performance Metrics, and iOS privacy-driven Signal Loss.\n**Procedures/Strategies:** Cautious budget allocation and efficiency-first testing; shifting optimization targets from raw downloads to user payment propensity; treating Android and iOS as distinct environments with customized creatives, bidding, and evaluation; implementing channel diversification as a risk management necessity; establishing rapid creative testing workflows (prioritizing short-form/UGC over static content) with high-frequency refresh cycles; adopting MTA and modeled conversions to allocate budgets across the full funnel and align int..."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0057",
    "title": "2026 MOBILE GAMES FULL-FUNNEL FRAMEWORK全球手游全链路增长策略指南04",
    "page_range": "40",
    "summary": "This section functions solely as a structural heading. It can answer questions regarding the primary topic (mobile game growth strategies), key entity (mobile games), and target year (2026). It introduces the \"Full-Funnel Framework\" as a strategic model but contains no definitions, procedures, claims, tables, figures, metrics, or evidence types. Retrieval queries should treat this node as a section marker for subsequent content on global mobile game full-link growth strategies rather than a source of detailed analytical data."
  }
]

message.content: This section analyzes the 2025 mobile game user acquisition (UA) market, documenting its strategic shift from an "emergency state" to a phase of "self-discipline and standardization" focused on sustainable, value-driven growth. **Topics** covered include quarterly performance dynamics (Q1–Q4, with emphasis on H2 and the Q4 holiday season), genre-specific scale and monetization divergence, cross-platform (Android vs. iOS) marketing strategies, digital ad budget allocation, channel diversification, creative optimization, and advertising measurement maturity. **Entities** referenced are mobile game advertisers/publishers, top brands, data provider Singular, Android, iOS, Paid Social platforms, and game genres (casual, hybrid-casual, mid-core, strategy). **Dates/Timeframes** span the full 2025 calendar year, with strategic planning insights extending into 2026. **Definitions/Concepts** detail the "rational growth path," value-centric optimization methodology, channel over-concentration risk, attribution frameworks (Last-Click vs. Multi-Touch Attribution/MTA), Assist Value, Cross-Channel Impact, Modeled Conversions, Hybrid Performance Metrics, and iOS privacy-driven Signal Loss. **Procedures/Strategies** outline efficiency-first budget allocation and testing, shifting optimization targets from raw downloads to early user payment propensity, implementing platform-specific creative/bidding/evaluation workflows for Android and iOS, enforcing channel diversification as a risk management requirement, establishing rapid creative testing pipelines (prioritizing short-form/UGC with high-frequency refresh cycles), and adopting MTA with modeled conversions for full-funnel budget alignment. **Claims** emphasize that treating Android and iOS as distinct environments and prioritizing payment propensity over volume are critical for modern UA success. **Tables, figures, specific quantitative metrics, and explicit evidence types** are not detailed within this parent segment. The section closes with a structural heading for the "2026 Mobile Games Full-Funnel Framework," serving as a navigational marker for subsequent content on global mobile game full-link growth strategies.
